sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Nothing has changed after multiple clicks. Let me reconsider the problem completely. Maybe I'm clicking on the wrong things.
Looking at the image again:
- There are 4 distinct 3x3 grids
- The bottom-right grid is inside a grey frame
- Each grid has a "center" cell that's different (has white/grey/red sub-elements)
- There's an orange bar at the very bottom
The fact that nothing changes when I click suggests I might be clicking on the wrong coordinates or the wrong type of element.
Let me look more carefully at the structure. The grids appear to be:
- Each cell in the 3x3 grid is about 6x6 pixels (36 pixels = 6x6)
- The center cell has a special 2x2 pattern inside
Wait - maybe the center cells contain small interactive elements. Let me look at the center of the top-left grid more carefully. The center cell (row 10-15, col 12-17) has:
- id=8: grey, 4px, center (10,12) - top-left corner of center
- id=9: white, 4px, center (10,14) - top of center  
- id=10: grey, 8px, center (11,16) - right side
- id=17: white, 4px, center (12,12) - left side
- id=18: red, 4px, center (12,14) - very center
- id=22: grey, 4px, center (14,12) - bottom-left
- id=23: white, 8px, center (14,15) - bottom
So the center cell has a complex pattern. The red pixel at (12,14) is the very center.
Let me try clicking on the orange bar at the bottom (id=64, 64 pixels, center at (63,31)). That's at row 63, which is the bottom row.
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe I need to click on specific colored blocks to change their color. The goal might be to make all 4 grids match the target (bottom-right grid which is all blue).
Let me try clicking on a red block to see if it changes to blue.

sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# id=64, color=O, 64 pixels, center=(63,31) - this is likely a timer/progress bar
# Let me also try clicking on a red block to see if it changes color

# First try the orange bar
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 63, 'col': 31}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
